'''Daniel Frame''' (born 7 June 1975) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played for the [[Melbourne Storm]] in 1998, then went over to [[England]] to play with the [[Widnes Vikings]] from 2002 to 2005.

==Playing career==
Originally from the [[Central Coast (New South Wales)|Central Coast]], Frame played his junior football with [[Wyong Roos]].<ref name="2006bio"/>

A {{rlp|lf}}, Frame made his first grade debut for [[Melbourne Storm]] in round 15 of the [[1998 NRL season]] against [[North Queensland Cowboys]]. He would play seven matches for the club in 1998, and was part of a minor controversy when caught on the sidelines during a match wearing a paper mask of then suspended teammate [[Rodney Howe]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Storm in a teacup |work=Newcastle Herald |publisher=Newcastle Newspapers Pty Limited |date=11 August 1998 |location=Newcastle, New South Wales |page=62}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Roach |first1=Stewart |title=Real culprit masked in Howe incident |work=Newcastle Herald |publisher=Newcastle Newspapers Pty Limited |date=12 August 1998 |location=Newcastle, New South Wales |page=91}}</ref> Playing for Melbourne's feeder club [[Norths Devils]], Frame was a member of the Devils [[1998 Queensland Cup]] winning team.

Frame remained with Melbourne during the [[1999 Melbourne Storm season|1999 season]], but only featured in matches for Norths Devils. Frame would play a representative game for the [[Queensland Residents rugby league team]] against NSW in 1999.<ref name="2006bio"/> He was released at the end of the 1999 season.<ref>{{cite news |title=A brave new world for NRL |work=Central Coast Herald |publisher=Fairfax Digital |date=29 January 2000 |pages=110–111}}</ref>

Moving to England, Frame played for [[Dewsbury Rams]] during the Rams [[2000 Northern Ford Premiership]] campaign, playing in the club's 13–12 win in the premiership final against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Dewsbury in last-gasp triumph |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/857468.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=27 January 2023 |date=29 July 2000}}</ref> After a season back in Australia with [[Wynnum-Manly Seagulls]], in late 2001 he joined [[Widnes Vikings]], after the Vikings were promoted to [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Aussie in Frame for the Vikings |work=Liverpool Echo |publisher=MGN Ltd |date=12 September 2001 |location=Liverpool, United Kingdom |page=57}}</ref>

Frame would go on to play four seasons with Widnes, scoring the club's first try in the [[Super League VII|2002 Super League season]].<ref name="2006bio">{{cite news |last1=Gardiner |first1=James |title=Right time frame for Daniel's new home |work=Newcastle Herald |publisher=Fairfax Digital |date=25 May 2006 |location=Newcastle, New South Wales |page=44}}</ref> He would qualify as a non-import player in 2003 after it was found that his mother was from Hull and Frame gained British citizenship.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Lawless |first1=John |title=Rugby League: Widnes: Frame not an overseas player |work=Liverpool Echo |publisher=MGN Ltd |date=23 July 2003 |location=Liverpool, United Kingdom |page=55}}</ref>

After Widnes were relegated from Super League in 2005, Frame returned to Australia, later playing in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]] competition with [[Lakes United]].<ref name="2006bio"/>
